parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: MathsTopic: Concept Introduction
Date: ___

Addition is one of the most basic arithmetic operations and is used to combine two or more digits/numbers together. Addends are the digits/numbers being added, and the result of the final answer we get after the process is called the sum. The symbol of addition is "+\".
Addition Statement is a mathematical expression that demonstrates two or more digits/numbers added together and their sum.
4+37 is an addition statement.
Subtraction is an arithmetic operation and is used to remove or take away objects or numbers from a given collection of objects or from another number. The symbol of addition is "-".
The number being subtracted is the subtraend, while the number it is subtracted from is the minuend. The result is the difference.
Word/Story Problem serves as a narrative tool that presents a real-world situation requiring a mathematical solution.
Times Table is a list of multiples of a number. Times table of any number can be obtained by adding the same number in every next step.
Cardinal numbers are numbers that are used for counting real objects or counting things. They are also known as "counting numbers" or "cardinals."
Ordinal numbers are numbers that are used to represent the rank or position of an object or a person. They are also referred to as positioning or ranking numbers.
Scanned with OKEN Scanner

Grade: 1Subject: MathsTopic: Concept Introduction
Date: ___ Addition is one of the most basic arithmetic operations and is used to combine two or more digits/numbers together. Addends are the digits/numbers being added, and the result of the final answer we get after the process is called the sum. The symbol of addition is "+\". Addition Statement is a mathematical expression that demonstrates two or more digits/numbers added together and their sum. 4+37 is an addition statement. Subtraction is an arithmetic operation and is used to remove or take away objects or numbers from a given collection of objects or from another number. The symbol of addition is "-". The number being subtracted is the subtraend, while the number it is subtracted from is the minuend. The result is the difference. Word/Story Problem serves as a narrative tool that presents a real-world situation requiring a mathematical solution. Times Table is a list of multiples of a number. Times table of any number can be obtained by adding the same number in every next step. Cardinal numbers are numbers that are used for counting real objects or counting things. They are also known as "counting numbers" or "cardinals." Ordinal numbers are numbers that are used to represent the rank or position of an object or a person. They are also referred to as positioning or ranking numbers.

parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: MathsTopic: Ordinal Numbers
Date ___
1. Follow the directions below and colour the circle band.
• Colour the second  $ (2^{nd}) $ circle RED.
• Colour the third  $ (3^{rd}) $ circle BLUE.
• Colour the first  $ 1^{st} $ circle ORANGE.
• Colour the fifth  $ 5^{\text{th}} $ circle GREEN.
• Colour the eight  $ 8^{th} $ circle PURPLE.
Don't colour the tenth  $ (10^{th}) $ circle.
• Colour the ninth  $ 9^{th} $ circle BROWN.
2. Read the problem. Place the ordinal number under each of the animal's name to show the place they came in.
The local animals had a race.
Ben Bear came first. Ernie Elephant came second. Charlie Chick came third. Abe Ape came fourth.
Abe Ape
Ben Bear
Charlie Chick
Ernie Elephant
Scanned with OKEN Scanner

Grade: 1Subject: MathsTopic: Ordinal Numbers
Date ___ 1. Follow the directions below and colour the circle band. • Colour the second $ (2^{nd}) $ circle RED. • Colour the third $ (3^{rd}) $ circle BLUE. • Colour the first $ 1^{st} $ circle ORANGE. • Colour the fifth $ 5^{\text{th}} $ circle GREEN. • Colour the eight $ 8^{th} $ circle PURPLE. Don't colour the tenth $ (10^{th}) $ circle. $$ (7^{\mathrm{t h}}) $$ • Colour the ninth $ 9^{th} $ circle BROWN.
2. Read the problem. Place the ordinal number under each of the animal's name to show the place they came in. The local animals had a race. Ben Bear came first. Ernie Elephant came second. Charlie Chick came third. Abe Ape came fourth.
Abe Ape Ben Bear Charlie Chick Ernie Elephant

parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: MathsTopic: Ordinal Numbers
Date: _____
Q1. Direction: Fill in the blanks with the given day of the week. Start with Monday as the  $ 1^{st} $ day of the week and complete the statements.
A. _____ is the first (1 $ ^{st} $) day of the week.
B. _____ is the second (2 $ ^{nd} $) day of the week.
C. _____ is the third (3 $ ^{rd} $) day of the week.
D. _____ is the fourth (4th) day of the week.
E. _____ is the fifth (5th) day of the week.
F. _____ is the sixth (6th) day of the week.
G. _____ is the seventh (7th) day of the week.

FridaySaturdayThursdayTuesday
SundayMondayWednesday
Q2. Look at the line of children playing FOLLOW THE LEADER. Ryan is the leader.
a) Who is in the tenth place?_____
b) Which place is Mindy in?_____
c) If Tony and Tom leave the line, what place would Mindy be in? _____
d) If Lou moves ahead in front Suzy what place is she now? _____

☑
TonyTomMindyLouEricSuzyJimMary
